title: Nematanthus bradei
taxonomy: Nematanthus bradei is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is part of the genus Nematanthus, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The family Gesneriaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as African violet family or gesneriad family. The Gesneriaceae family is part of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is a subset of the angiosperm clade, which includes most flowering plants in the world.

intro: Nematanthus bradei, also known as Brazilian Gloxinia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Nematanthus bradei include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nematanthus bradei include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
